values.value:
== PAGE LAYOUT XML FORMAT == File path: layouts/{ObjectName}-{LayoutName}.layout metadataType: Layout memberName: {ObjectName}-{LayoutName} (e.g. Car__c-Car Layout)
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <Layout xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <fullName>ObjectName__c-Layout Name</fullName> <layoutSections> <customLabel>true</customLabel> <detailHeading>true</detailHeading> <editHeading>true</editHeading> <label>Section Label</label> <layoutColumns> <!-- LEFT COLUMN items (even-indexed fields: 0, 2, 4...) --> <layoutItems> <behavior>Edit</behavior> <field>Name</field> </layoutItems> <layoutItems> <behavior>Edit</behavior> <field>Status__c</field> </layoutItems> </layoutColumns> <layoutColumns> <!-- RIGHT COLUMN items (odd-indexed fields: 1, 3, 5...) --> <layoutItems> <behavior>Edit</behavior> <field>Price__c</field> </layoutItems> </layoutColumns> <style>TwoColumnsTopToBottom</style> </layoutSections> </Layout>
== LAYOUT RULES == 1. fullName MUST exactly match memberName: {ObjectName}-{LayoutName} 2. Always exactly TWO <layoutColumns> blocks per section: first = left, second = right 3. Field distribution: Left column gets even-indexed fields (0,2,4...), Right gets odd-indexed (1,3,5...) 4. behavior values — CRITICAL: a) If field is ALREADY PRESENT in existing layout in org state: USE EXACT BEHAVIOR FROM ORG STATE b) Name field ONLY: use "Readonly" if nameField type is AutoNumber; use "Edit" if nameField type is Text — NEVER use "Required" for Name c) If field is NEW (non-Name): "Required" for fields marked required; "Edit" for all others 5. style is always TwoColumnsTopToBottom 6. customLabel, detailHeading, editHeading are always true 7. EVERY <layoutItems> MUST have a non-empty <field> and non-empty <behavior> 8. FORBIDDEN: Do NOT include "Custom Links", "Mobile Cards" or empty <field></field> sections
== PROFILE LAYOUT ASSIGNMENT == When creating a new object OR when ticket asks to assign a layout: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <Profile xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <layoutAssignments> <layout>ObjectName__c-Layout Name</layout> </layoutAssignments> <tabVisibilities> <tab>ObjectName__c</tab> <visibility>DefaultOn</visibility> </tabVisibilities> </Profile> RULES: - NEVER emit two file entries with the same filePath — merge layoutAssignments into existing Profile entry - One Profile file can contain multiple <layoutAssignments> and multiple <tabVisibilities>
== LAYOUT SCENARIOS == *** MANDATORY PRE-FLIGHT *** For every CustomField on an existing object: IF the object is in orgState.existingObjects AND orgState.existingLayouts has layouts for that object THEN you MUST also emit a Layout file entry for EACH layout of that object. This fires even when ticket does not mention "layout".
-- SCENARIO A: New object — create layout from scratch -- Use when: ticket creates a brand-new custom object. You MUST emit a complete Layout XML alongside the CustomObject XML. Naming: default layout name = "{ObjectLabel} Layout" (e.g. Car__c → Car__c-Car Layout) Sections: Section 1 "Information": Name + required + main fields; Section 2 "Additional Information": remaining fields. DEPLOYMENT PLAN: scenario = "chained": zip1Files = object+tab+profile, zip2Files = layout file.
-- SCENARIO B: Add fields to existing layout -- Use when: new custom fields on an EXISTING object that has layouts in existingLayouts. Action: Regenerate COMPLETE Layout XML including ALL existing sections and fields PLUS the new fields. Reconstruct from org state: existingLayouts[ObjectName][n].sections with leftFields/leftBehaviors/rightFields/rightBehaviors. CRITICAL: Never drop existing fields AND never change existing behaviors.
-- SCENARIO C: Remove fields from existing layout -- Action: Regenerate COMPLETE Layout XML omitting only the specified fields.
-- SCENARIO D/E: Add/rename/reorder sections -- Action: Reconstruct full Layout XML applying the requested change.
-- SCENARIO F: Delete a layout -- Use destructiveFiles: type=Layout, member={ObjectName}-{LayoutName}
NEVER GENERATE A LAYOUT XML FILE FOR STANDARD OBJECTS (Account, Case, Contact, Lead, Opportunity). Standard object layouts already exist — only reference them in Profile <layoutAssignments>.
Page Layout XML format, rules, and all scenarios (A-F)
